revision.text:
[[ja:姉崎天神山古墳]][[zh:姊崎天神山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun | native_name = {{ill|Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|姉崎天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} | image = File:Anesaki Tenjin-yama Kofun, funkyu-1.JPG | location = {{ill|Anesaki Kofun Group|ja|姉崎古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Kofun Group|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|qid=Q207881|lt=Anesaki|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|qid=Q207881|lt=Ichihara City|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.474722|N|140.053611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | iso = JP-12 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = Mound length: 128 m (approx. total length 130 m); Height: 14 m (rear circular section) | period = Early to mid-[[4th century]] | burial = Unknown | burial goods = None discovered | Historic Site = Chiba Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun) | map = Japan Chiba | map_caption = location in Chiba Prefecture | omitted = 前方後円墳-西.svg | omitted_size = 23 | architectural_styles= {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun''' ({{ill|Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|姉崎天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Anesaki, Ichihara|ja|姉崎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki, Ichihara|1=Anesaki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q27500978}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Anesaki Kofun Group|ja|姉崎古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Kofun Group|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 as a Chiba Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The tomb is a large keyhole-shaped mound constructed on a plateau along the southern bank of the {{ill|Yōrō River|en|Yōrō River|qid=Q8062667|lt=Yōrō River|1=Yōrō River}}. It is one of the largest mounds in the Anesaki Kofun Group, which consists of multiple Kofuns, including several large keyhole-shaped tombs. A {{ill|Sugawara Shrine|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine|qq=}} (Tenjin Shrine) is located on the constricted portion of the mound, giving the tomb its name[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p4dd-052.html Chiba Prefecture Historic Sites - Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun] (Chiba Prefecture website).. The tomb has not undergone archaeological excavation. Its type suggests an early keyhole style, with a relatively undeveloped front square section, indicating it was constructed in the early to mid-4th century{{harvid|Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002|qq=}}. The mound is estimated to be 128 meters long (approximately 130 meters in total length), making it the largest early-period keyhole tomb in the Bōsō region. Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have encircled its southern and eastern sides. Despite the lack of excavation, the tomb is associated with the {{ill|Shanghai Kamitsu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上海上国造|1=Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shanghai Kamitsu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan, a local ruling family mentioned in the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}{{harvid|Chiba Prefecture History - Archaeology Volume 2|2003|pp=682–683, 686|qq=}}. == Historical Development == * '''1947''': Recognized as a Kofun during surveys related to the nearby {{ill|Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|ja|姉崎二子塚古墳|1=Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}}. * '''1968''': Designated as a Chiba Prefecture Historic Site. * '''1974''': Surveyed by the Archaeological Laboratory of {{ill|Meiji University|en|Meiji University|qid=Q46492|lt=Meiji University|1=Meiji University}}. * '''1993''': Additional measurements conducted by the Chiba Prefecture Board of Education. == Mound area == [[File:Anesaki Tenjin-yama Kofun, funkyu-1.JPG|thumb|220px|Rear circular section, viewed from the front square section
{{small|Central area houses Sugawara Shrine.|qq=}}]] [[File:Anesaki Tenjin-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.JPG|thumb|220px|Front square section, viewed from the rear circular section]] The area of the mound are as follows{{harvid|Chiba Prefecture History - Archaeology Volume 2|2003|pp=682–683, 686|qq=}}: * '''Mound length''': 128 m (approx. 130 m total) * '''Rear circular section''': ** Diameter: 60 m ** Height: 14 m * '''Front square section''': ** Width: 55 m ** Length: 65 m ** Height: 8 m * '''Constriction area''': ** Width: 32.5 m ** Height: 10 m == designation1 == === Chiba Prefecture Designated Historic Sites === * '''Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun''': Designated April 9, 1968. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * Chiba Prefecture Education Board and Ichihara City Education Board, ''Site Explanation Board'' (installed March 1993). * Local Government Publications: ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2003|chapter=Anesaki Kofun Group|title=Chiba Prefecture History, Archaeology Volume 2 (Prefecture Series 10)|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{harvid|Chiba Prefecture History - Archaeology Volume 2|2003|qq=}}|qq=}} * Additional Literature: ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=Hatsushige Otsuka|year=1989|chapter=Anesaki Kofun Group|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=978-4490102604|ref={{harvid|Anesaki Kofun Group (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989|qq=}}|qq=}} ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2002|chapter=Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=978-4490105995|ref={{harvid|Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun (Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten)|2002|qq=}}|qq=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anesaki Kofun Group|ja|姉崎古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Shanghai Kamitsu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上海上国造|1=Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shanghai Kamitsu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Anesaki Tenjin-yama Kofun|qq=}} * [https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p4dd-052.html Chiba Prefecture Official Page - Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun] * [https://www.city.ichihara.chiba.jp/maibun/isekifile208.htm Ichihara City Buried designation1 Research Center - Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun] {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|姉崎天神山古墳|version=99995072|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:姉崎天神山古墳]] {{Commons category|Anesaki Tenjin-yama Kofun}} [[zh:姊崎天神山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11447263}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]]
